ORDER ON DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT (OVERRULED; 10 DAYS TO ANSWER)
MINI MINUTES FOR OCT-07-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT GLENDA CASHMAN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED; 10 DAYS TO ANSWER. IN THE ABSENCE OF A PROBATE PROCEEDING OR TRUST NOTICE PROCEDURES, THE RIGHTS OF A CREDITOR OF THE DECEDENT AGAINST THE ASSETS OF THE DECEDENT'S REVOCABLE TRUST ARE "AS OTHERWISE PROVIDED BY LAW" (CAL. PROB. CODE 19008) ON LIABILITY ARISING FROM CONTRACT (19000(C) AND (A)(1)). THAT INCLUDES THE RIGHT TO PROCEED AGAINST TRUST ASSETS TO THE EXTENT THE PROBATE ESTATE IS INADEQUATE. (19001(A)) THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES THE NECESSARY FACTS TO BRING THE CLAIM WITHIN 19001 AND 19008. EVEN ASSUMING THE 1 YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS UNDER CCP 336.2 APPLIES, PLAINTIFF'S ACTION WAS BROUGHT WITHIN ONE YEAR. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

THE COURT DENIED PLAINTIFF'S ORAL REQUEST TO CONTINUE THE TRIAL DATE. THE COURT ORDERED THE CASE DISMISSED WITHOUT PREJUDICE FOR NOT BEING READY TO GO TO TRIAL. JUDGE: CYNTHIA M. LEE; CLERK: R. VELUZ; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar May-27-2014 Dismissal - No Appearance

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-23-2014 Set For Trial On May-27-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-07-2013 Overruled
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: MATTHEW KUMAR, ESQ., 818.707.2733, FOR PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO N.A.
CIARAN O'SULILVAN, ESQ., 415.391.3711, FOR DEFENDNAT GLENDA CASHMAN AS SUCCESSOR TRUSTEE OF THE YOLANDA C. BATTAD SURVIVOR TRUST.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT GLENDA CASHMAN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled; 10 days to answer. In the absence of a probate proceeding or trust notice procedures, the rights of a creditor of the decedent against the assets of the decedent's revocable trust are "as otherwise provided by law" (Cal. Prob. Code 19008) on liability arising from contract (19000(c) and (a)(1)). That includes the right to proceed against trust assets to the extent the probate estate is inadequate. (19001(a)) The Complaint alleges the necessary facts to bring the claim within 19001 and 19008. Even assuming the 1 year statute of limitations under CCP 336.2 applies, Plaintiff's action was brought within one year. Order signed in open court. Judge: Peter J. Busch; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185
